steps.title: Translate the speed constraints to mathematical inequalities
steps.content:
The phrase at least 40 miles per hour means the speed is greater than or equal to 40, so $x \geq 40$.

The phrase no more than 65 miles per hour means the speed is less than or equal to 65, so $x \leq 65$.

Since the car must satisfy both conditions simultaneously, we need to combine them into a single compound inequality.

steps.title: Write the compound inequality
steps.content: When a value must be greater than or equal to one number AND less than or equal to another, we write both inequalities together: $40 \leq x \leq 65$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
